# puru (プール)

> Go-style concurrency primitives for JavaScript worker threads

`puru` is a zero-dependency thread pool for JavaScript. It runs work off the main thread with inline worker functions, no worker files, and no manual message plumbing.

Supported runtimes:
- Node.js >= 20
- Bun

## Install

```bash
npm install @dmop/puru
# or
bun add @dmop/puru
```

## Best Fit

Use `puru` when a user needs:
- CPU-bound work off the main thread
- Go-style channels, `WaitGroup`, `ErrGroup`, `select`, or `context`
- worker threads without separate worker files
- parallel pipelines or structured concurrency in Node.js or Bun

Do not reach for `puru` first when:
- plain `Promise.all()` is enough
- the work is trivial and finishes in under ~5ms
- the runtime has no real thread support

## Core API

- `spawn(fn, opts?)` — run one self-contained function in a worker thread; returns `{ result, cancel }`
- `task(fn)` — reusable worker-thread function with explicit JSON-serializable arguments
- `chan(capacity?)` — Go-style channels with `send`, `recv`, `close`, async iteration, `len`, `cap`, and directional views
- `WaitGroup(ctx?)` — run many tasks and wait for all
- `ErrGroup(ctx?)` — fail-fast group that cancels the rest on first error
- `select(cases, opts?)` — wait for the first ready send, recv, or timer case
- `background()`, `withCancel()`, `withTimeout()`, `withDeadline()`, `withValue()` — Go-style context helpers
- `Mutex`, `RWMutex`, `Cond`, `Once`
- `after()`, `ticker()`, `Timer`
- `configure()`, `stats()`, `resize()`, `shutdown()`

## Execution Modes

- Default exclusive mode: one task per worker, best for CPU-heavy work
- `{ concurrent: true }`: many tasks share a worker event loop, best for async and I/O-heavy work

## Critical Rule

Functions passed to `spawn()` are serialized via `.toString()` and sent to a worker. They must be self-contained.

Wrong:

```ts
const x = 42
spawn(() => x + 1)
```

Right:

```ts
spawn(() => {
  const x = 42
  return x + 1
})
```

## Constraints

- `spawn()` functions cannot capture outer variables
- `task()` arguments must be JSON-serializable
- channel values must be structured-cloneable
- `null` is reserved as the channel-closed sentinel
- spawn overhead is roughly `0.1-0.5ms`; use it for tasks above roughly `5ms`

## Links

- README: https://github.com/dmop/puru#readme
- Full LLM docs: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmop/puru/main/llms-full.txt
- AI assistant guide: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmop/puru/main/AGENTS.md
